title: AGI is Here. Anthropic Just Proved It.
summary: Nate Herk argues that AGI has already arrived based on Anthropic's report showing 80% of their code is now written by AI. The video examines Anthropic's internal data on recursive self-improvement, explores three possible futures, and discusses why Anthropic is warning about AI risks despite building advanced systems.
writeup: This video walks through Anthropic's internal report 'When AI Builds Itself,' using Anthropic's own numbers to argue that practical AGI (defined as open-ended, self-directed problem-solving rather than narrow task execution) has already arrived. The through-line is a cascade of internal benchmarks: Claude's success rate on open-ended engineering problems jumped from 26% to 76% in six months, autonomous task duration has been doubling every four months, and on a head-to-head test across 129 decision points, the model now outpicks human researchers 64% of the time. The video closes on the alignment risk: if the AI is building its own successor, small misalignment errors compound and become less visible with each generation.
key_points: Anthropic's internal data shows Claude's success rate on open-ended coding tasks (no specification, no known answer) rose from 26% to 76% in six months, the metric the video treats as the meaningful AGI threshold.

The maximum task duration an Anthropic model can handle autonomously has gone from 4 minutes two years ago to over 12 hours today, with the length roughly doubling every four months; Anthropic projects tasks taking a person weeks could fall in range by 2027.
On 129 frozen research decision points, Claude chose the better next step than the human researcher 51% of the time in November 2024 and 64% by April 2025.

A single model optimization pass on a training code block achieved a 52x speedup in April 2025 versus 3x a year earlier; on a problem human researchers had worked for a week (reaching 23% of the gap), AI agents running continuously clawed back 97%.

Anthropic's report sketches three scenarios: plateau, compounding gains with humans still directing, and the AI building its own successor. The video argues scenario two is the present reality and scenario three is the open question.

Anthropic explicitly states that rare misalignment in today's models could compound as each model builds its successor, growing more frequent and less understood, which is the core safety concern the report surfaces.
